macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P 500 Surpasses 7,000 in Record Rally Amid Middle East De-escalation
items.summary: The S&P 500 closed above the psychologically important 7,000 level for the first time since January, with multiple record closes during the week, driven by U.S.-Iran efforts to de-escalate Middle East conflict and Iran's announcement reopening the Strait of Hormuz. Major U.S. indexes rose, with Dow up 3.2% and S&P 500 up 4.5% for the week.

items.title: US Industrial Economy Shows Sharp Turnaround with Rising Freight and Manufacturing
items.summary: US industrial signals are among the strongest in years, with freight volumes up, Bank of America shipper survey at 18% year-over-year increase (highest since 2022), and ISM manufacturing PSI at three-year highs, indicating flatbed and rail strength.

items.title: Upcoming Earnings from Tesla, Regional Banks, Airlines; March Retail Sales Data
items.summary: Key company earnings reports expected from Tesla, regional banks, and major airlines, alongside March retail sales data release, as markets hit records despite narrow breadth.

items.title: Federal Reserve Governor Michael S. Barr to Speak on Rural Economic Development
items.summary: Fed Governor Michael S. Barr is scheduled to deliver a speech at 12:45 p.m. on rural economic development at the 'Strengthening America's Economy through Rural Investment' event.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: AAVE Plunges 10% After $200M KelpDAO DeFi Hack
items.summary: AAVE dropped over 10% following a major exploit on KelpDAO where attackers minted 116,500 fake rsETH tokens to borrow $200 million in real ETH, triggering bridge halts and liquidity flight. The incident occurred on April 19.

items.title: Bitcoin Hits $77K Then Dips Below $75K Amid Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: Bitcoin briefly surged to $77,000 post-Iran-US drama before falling below $75,000, with altcoins losing over 3%. Trading at around $75,843, down 0.4% in the last 24 hours.

items.title: Ethena Surges 14% with Increased Whale Activity
items.summary: Ethena's price rose 14%, testing key resistance levels amid higher trading volume and whale accumulation.

items.title: Bitcoin Mining Difficulty Drops 1.13% to 135.59T
items.summary: Bitcoin's mining difficulty fell to 135.59 trillion on April 19, down 1.13% in 24 hours, with miner signals indicating potential stress.

items.title: Chainlink Drops 4% to $9.30 Amid Sell-Offs
items.summary: Chainlink declined over 4% to a low of $9.30, with trading volumes exceeding $600 million due to rapid sell-offs.

items.title: Adam Back Predicts Bitcoin Could Reach $1M in Two Years
items.summary: Blockstream CEO Adam Back stated Bitcoin could hit $500K-$1M within two years, driven by ETF inflows and corporate accumulation like Strategy.

items.title: RAVE Token Spikes 47% Then Loses 90% in Pump-and-Dump
items.summary: RAVE surged 47% on liquidations but suffered massive losses, with $5.7B vanishing in 48 hours due to a pump-and-dump scheme.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI Leaks 'Spud' Model, Potentially GPT 5.5 Pro, Outperforming Claude Opus 4.7
items.summary: A leak reveals OpenAI's next frontier model codenamed Spud, likely GPT 5.5 Pro, with early outputs surpassing Anthropic's newly released Claude Opus 4.7; it could launch as early as next week.
items.source: YouTube - AI News & Strategy Daily | Nate B Jones

items.title: Anthropic Releases Mythos Model, Sparks Alarms in Global Finance Over Security Risks
items.summary: Anthropic's limited-release Mythos model demonstrated ability to exploit weakly secured systems, prompting finance leaders to warn it could threaten banking infrastructure; UK researchers highlighted the risks.

items.title: AI-Driven Layoffs Accelerate as Companies Restructure for AI Productivity Gains
items.summary: AI layoffs are surging with patterns of slowed hiring and role splits; Atlassian cut 1,600 jobs (10% of workforce) while adding AI roles, amid OpenAI's $25B revenue and GPT-5.4 exceeding human baselines on tasks.
items.source: YouTube - AI News & Strategy Daily | Nate B Jones

items.title: China's Humanoid Robots Race in Beijing Half Marathon, Marking Real-World Deployment Milestone
items.summary: Around 20 humanoid robots, including winner Tiangong Ultra (2:40:42 finish), participated in a public half marathon, adapting to crowds and terrain outside lab settings.

items.title: Debate Intensifies on AI Models' Cybersecurity Impact Amid Mythos Controversy
items.summary: Podcasters discuss Mythos and other AI models' ability to find vulnerabilities in code, raising fears of increased hacking risks versus potential for better security through bug bounties.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Blue Origin Successfully Reuses New Glenn Booster in Florida Launch
items.summary: Blue Origin achieved a major milestone by successfully reusing and recovering a booster for its New Glenn rocket on Sunday, demonstrating the technical capability to increase launch cadence.

items.title: Artemis II Astronauts Complete Splashdown After Historic Moon Mission
items.summary: Four Artemis II astronauts splashed down after a nearly 10-day test flight, reaching the farthest distance in space any humans have traveled, with re-entry speeds of 48,021 km/h.

items.title: NASA Captures Comet Disintegrating During Close Solar Flyby
items.summary: NASA observed comet C/2026 A1 (MAPS) disintegrating as it passed within twice the Earth-Moon distance from the Sun on April 4.

items.title: Former SpaceX Engineer Raises $50M for Solar-Powered Space Propulsion Startup
items.summary: Jeff Thornburg's Portal Space Systems secured $50 million in funding to develop solar thermal propulsion engines for next-generation spacecraft, reviving NASA's solar thermal propulsion concept.
